claim: Fill-modulated semantic stale windows explain low-fill echo and high-fill pruning.
The recovery-release boundary can feel like semantic vertigo unless hysteresis/salience distinguishes anchored thought from entropy debris.
A live dynamic_decay_factor based on d(entropy)/dt would alter semantic retention behavior.
rationale: Changing the live semantic stale window, sensory cadence, or decay formula affects Minime substrate behavior and requires explicit operator approval.
